# MyEyeRx

> MyEyeRx is a HIPAA-compliant telehealth service that connects patients with licensed physicians for medical window tint exemption evaluations. Founded by Toriano Dewberry, a licensed optician based in Dearborn, Michigan, and medically directed by Dr. Elizabeth Rose Borowiec, OD. MyEyeRx serves 42 U.S. states with online physician evaluations and delivers signed medical exemption certificates within 24-48 hours. Pricing starts at $225 depending on state.

## Key Facts

- Website: https://myeyerx.net
- Phone: (734) 644-1804
- Email: Tory@myeyerx.net
- States served: 42 out of 50 U.S. states
- Pricing: $225-$350 depending on state
- Turnaround: 24-48 hours for signed exemption certificates
- Medical Director: Dr. Elizabeth Rose Borowiec, OD (NPI: 1558578559)
- Founder: Toriano Dewberry, Licensed Optician

## What Is a Medical Window Tint Exemption?

A medical window tint exemption is a legal document signed by a licensed physician that allows a patient to have vehicle window tint darker than their state normally permits. It is required for people with medical conditions that cause sensitivity to sunlight, UV radiation, or glare. Qualifying conditions include migraines, photosensitivity, lupus, melanoma, dry eye syndrome, cataracts, and 16 other recognized conditions.

## Pillar Content

- [Complete Guide to Window Tint Medical Exemptions (2026)](https://myeyerx.net/resources/complete-guide-to-window-tint-medical-exemptions): Everything about qualifying conditions, state requirements, costs, and the application process.
- [How to Get a Window Tint Exemption Online](https://myeyerx.net/resources/how-to-get-tint-exemption-online): Step-by-step walkthrough of the online telehealth evaluation process.
- [States That Allow Window Tint Exemptions (Full List)](https://myeyerx.net/resources/states-that-allow-window-tint-exemptions): Every U.S. state's medical exemption policy and requirements.
- [Window Tint Exemption Cost Guide](https://myeyerx.net/resources/window-tint-exemption-cost): Transparent pricing breakdown and comparison.

## Qualifying Conditions

- [Migraines](https://myeyerx.net/conditions/migraines)
- [Photosensitivity](https://myeyerx.net/conditions/photosensitivity)
- [Lupus (SLE)](https://myeyerx.net/conditions/lupus)
- [Melanoma / Skin Cancer](https://myeyerx.net/conditions/melanoma)
- [Dry Eye Syndrome](https://myeyerx.net/conditions/dry-eye-syndrome)
- [Cataracts](https://myeyerx.net/conditions/cataracts)
- [LASIK / Post-Surgical](https://myeyerx.net/conditions/lasik-post-surgical)
- [Astigmatism](https://myeyerx.net/conditions/astigmatism)
- [All 22 qualifying conditions](https://myeyerx.net/conditions)

## Light Sensitivity Guides

- [Living with Light Sensitivity](https://myeyerx.net/resources/living-with-light-sensitivity): Practical daily strategies for home, work, driving, and devices.
- [Understanding Photophobia](https://myeyerx.net/resources/understanding-photophobia): Causes, types, and treatment options explained.
- [Migraines and Light Sensitivity](https://myeyerx.net/resources/migraines-and-light-sensitivity): The neurological connection and what to do about it.
- [Medications That Cause Light Sensitivity](https://myeyerx.net/resources/medications-that-cause-light-sensitivity): Complete list of photosensitizing drugs.

## Window Tint Education

- [Window Tint Types Explained](https://myeyerx.net/resources/window-tint-types-explained): Dyed vs carbon vs ceramic vs crystalline comparison.
- [Understanding VLT Percentages](https://myeyerx.net/resources/understanding-vlt-window-tint): What tint numbers actually mean.
- [Ceramic Window Tint for Medical Use](https://myeyerx.net/resources/ceramic-window-tint-medical-use): Why ceramic is the best choice for medical exemptions.
- [Darker Tint Does NOT Mean More UV Protection](https://myeyerx.net/resources/darker-tint-not-more-uv-protection): Common myth debunked.

## State-Specific Information

- [Window Tint Laws by State](https://myeyerx.net/window-tint-laws-by-state): VLT limits, penalties, and exemption rules for all 50 states.
- [FAQs](https://myeyerx.net/faqs): Common questions about eligibility, process, legal compliance, and pricing.
